title: Nuclei Isolation and Clean-Up using GentleMACS and Anti-Nucleus Microbeads
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5lnc4","text":"Obtaining a clean nuclei suspension for subsequent single-nuclei RNA sequencing can be challenging. Especially liver nuclei suspensions are prone to high ambient RNA content.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8cn55","text":"Alternative nuclei clean up methods like Fluorescence-activated Cell Sorting can stress nuclei, decrease nuclei quality and are time consuming and need specialized equipment. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2jrip","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fqv33","text":"To overcome these challenges we applied a simple protocol for isolating and cleaning up nuclei for single-nuclei RNA sequencing using the GentleMACS Octo dissociator coupled with anti-Nucleus microbeads cleanup.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f9v2t","text":"The following protocol has been successfully tested for Rat, Mouse and Minipig liver as well as Mouse brain. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

130-042-303)\nRefrigerated Centrifuge ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eichc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9mc4g","text":"Anti-Nucleus MicroBeads (Miltenyi,# 130-132-997)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fa06m","text":"Nuclei Extraction Buffer (Mliltenyi# 130-128-024) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2mi4m","text":"MACS BSA Stock Solution (# 130-091-376) or BSa 30% to be checked","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fl3e7","text":"MACS SmartStrainers (70 µm) (# 130-098-462) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f6oqm","text":"MACS SmartStrainers (30 µm) (# 130-098-458) or CellTrics (Sysmex, #04-004-2326)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ctb09","text":"gentleMACS C Tubes (# 130-093-237, # 130-096-334) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"427k","text":"LS Columns (# 130-042-401) ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4bpn3","text":"RNase inhibitor (e.g. RiboLock RNase Inhibitor )","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"anp5a","text":"Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), pH 7.2 ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"522243FD96B611EFBD500A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}},"1":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"65FDF4CC96B611EFBD500A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}}}}
steps: Pre-cool the buffers, and consumables with sample contact (e.g. gentleMACS C Tube and SmartStrainers) at 4 °C Overnight Place the gentleMACS Octo Coolers in -20 °C Overnight
Pre-cool the centrifuge to 4 °C before the start of the experiments
Per extraction, add RNase inhibitor (final concentration 0.2 U/μL ) to pre-cooled 4 mL Nuclei Extraction Buffer.

Prepare Nuclei separation buffer (for resuspension) before further enrichment of the nuclei using Anti-Nucleus MicroBeads. Per sample: 1x PBS: 10 ml Nuclei Extraction Buffer: 1667 μL BSA (30%): 15.6 μL RNAse inhibitor (0.2U/ul): 58.3 μL Degas buffer before use, as air bubbles could block the column.
In a pre-cooled Petri dish on dry ice, cut a fresh frozen tissue sample into a piece of 25-50 mg with a pre-cooled scalpel. Make sure that the sample is always on dry ice.
Add 2 mL ice-cold lysis buffer to each pre-cooled gentleMACS C Tube.
Transfer tissue pieces to the gentleMACS C Tube containing lysis buffer
Close gentleMACS C Tube and place it on the gentleMACS Dissociator.
Retrieve gentleMACS Octo Coolers form the freezer and attach them over the C tubes on the GentleMACs
Run gentleMACS Program 4C_nuclei_1 on the gentleMACS Dissociator. Detach the tube after the program finished and place it on ice
Apply nuclei suspension to a MACS SmartStrainer (70 µm) (from brain tissue samples : 100uM) placed on a 5 mL tube
Wash MACS SmartStrainer with 2 mL ice-cold lysis buffer
Discard MACS SmartStrainer and centrifuge nuclei suspension at 30 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Carefully aspirate supernatant completely.
Resuspend nuclei pellet with 1.5 ml ice-cold resuspension buffer by slowly and gently pipetting the sample up and down for 10 times
Apply nuclei suspension to a Celltrics Filter (30 μm)on a 1.5 mL tube or a MACS SmartStrainer (30μm) placed on a 5 mL tube.
Determine the nucleus number preferably using an automated Cell Counter
Transfer the volume that corresponds to 2 million nuclei to a new 1.5ml Eppi and centrifuge 300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes . Carefully remove supernatant
Resuspend nucleus pellet in 900 µL of nuclei separation buffer and transfer to 5ml tube
Add 100 µL of Anti-Nucleus MicroBeads
Mix well and incubate for 15 min 4 °C
Add 2 mL nuclei separation buffer and proceed to magnetic separation
Place LS column in the magnetic field of a suitable MACS Separator
Prepare column by rinsing with 3 mL of nuclei separation buffer
Apply nucleus suspension onto the column. Collect flowthrough containing debris
Wash column two times with 1 mL of nuclei separation buffer. Collect debris that passes through and combine with the flow-through from previous step
Remove column from the separator and place it on a 1.5ml Eppi
Pipette 1 mL nuclei separation buffer onto the column. Immediately flush out the magnetically labeled nuclei by firmly pushing the plunger into the column.
Centrifuge 300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes , remove supernatant
Resuspend the pellet in 500 µL of PBS + 0.04% BSA (with RNAse inhibitor at 0.2U/ul).
Determine the nuclei concentration preferably using an Fluorescence-based automated Cell Counter
Dilute the sample with PBS + 0.04% BSA to the recommended concentration for single nuclei RNA sequencing
creator: Fabian Köchl
